title: Mildly Fluffy Moroccan Rice Wrap
description: A yeasty, aerated wrap featuring fragrant basmati rice mixed with Moroccan spices and sweet dried fruit, served in warm spinach wraps for a lazy-sunday treat.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a saucepan combine the rinsed rice, 1.5 cups water, 1 tbsp olive oil, and 1/2 tsp salt; bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 16 minutes until the water is absorbed.
Meanwhile toast 2 tbsp sliced almonds in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, then set aside.
Whisk together ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, cinnamon, turmeric, lemon zest, lemon juice, and 1 tsp harissa paste to create a spice vinaigrette.
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the spice vinaigrette, chopped apricots, chopped figs, mint, cilantro, and toasted almonds, then let it cool slightly.
Warm spinach leaves in a hot skillet for 30 seconds on each side to make them pliable, then pat dry with a paper towel.
Place a spoonful of the flavored rice mixture onto the center of each spinach leaf, add a few slices of red onion, and roll the leaf into a wrap.
Serve the wraps immediately with a side of yogurt or a drizzle of tahini dressing.
